summary: Superb Charlie Miller soundboard capture of a landmark 4/23/69 Ark show – crystal‑clear mix, soaring Jerry solos and a standout Dark Star/He Was A Friend Of Mine set.
review: The recording is an audio masterpiece: a pristine soundboard transfer by Charlie Miller that reproduces the band’s 1969 energy with exceptional clarity, aside from a brief cut in the St. Stephen → It’s A Sin transition. Musically the performance ranks among the year’s best – a quiet, beautifully played He Was A Friend Of Mine opener, an epic Dark Star, long‑running Caution, the long‑awaited Morning Dew and a funky stretch of Hard To Handle, Alligator and The Eleven that keep the set fresh.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of detailed reviews award 5 stars, praising both the performance (standout Dark Star, He Was A Friend Of Mine, Morning Dew) and the flawless soundboard transfer; only a minor recording glitch is noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix with no noticeable hiss; praised as an "Audio Masterpiece" by multiple reviewers. One reviewer notes a brief splice/cut in the St. Stephen → It’s A Sin segment.

standout_songs: He Was A Friend Of Mine
show_quality.setlist_flow: A refreshing departure from the usual Dark Star → St. Stephen → Eleven jam format, with a folky opener that builds into extended jams (Hard To Handle, Alligator, The Eleven) and tight transitions throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised – "Jerry climbs like an eagle", perfect tone on Dark Star, and his banter adds to the show’s excitement.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ/harmonica work gets a shout‑out as a "Pigpen fix" that hits every spot.
